creator: crazyalexnt@inbox.ru
text:
> Hi,
> 
> Your USB 2.0 controller most likely has the lost interrupt bug. Try adding 
> your USB 2.0 controller to the list of devices quirked in:
> 
> src/sys/dev/usb/controller/ehci_pci.c:
> 
> /* Dropped interrupts workaround */
> switch (pci_get_vendor(self)) {
> case PCI_EHCI_VENDORID_ATI:
> case PCI_EHCI_VENDORID_VIA:
> sc->sc_flags |= EHCI_SCFLG_LOSTINTRBUG;
> if (bootverbose)
> device_printf(self,
> "Dropped interrupts workaround enabled\n");
> break;
> default:
> break;
> }

Hello

I adding my USB 2.0 controller to the list of devices quirked in:

         /* Dropped interrupts workaround */
         switch (pci_get_vendor(self)) {
         case PCI_EHCI_VENDORID_ATI:
         case PCI_EHCI_VENDORID_VIA:
         case PCI_EHCI_VENDORID_NVIDIA:
         case PCI_EHCI_VENDORID_NVIDIA2:
                 sc->sc_flags |= EHCI_SCFLG_LOSTINTRBUG;
                 if (bootverbose)
                         device_printf(self,
                             "Dropped interrupts workaround enabled\n");
                 break;
         default:
                 break;
         }

... and recompile the kernel. This correctly?
Then the problem has not disappeared, when copying from usb stick and usb hdd copy process
stops at the time from several seconds to several minutes.

I added in /boot/loader.conf options:
boot_verbose="YES"
debug.bootverbose="1"

I tried to work with the usb disks, here is my dmesg:

I immediately load the FreeBSD 7.2 and is actively working with the usb stick and usb hdd,
everything works fine. Hence, I conclude that the problem lies in the new USB subsystem of
FreeBSD 8.0.

Two of my friends motherboards based on chipset nForce, there has been a similar problem
with USB devices in FreeBSD 8.0. However, when working with a motherboard based on Intel
in FreeBSD 8.0 no problems there. From what I conclude that the blame for not working
correctly USB subsystem on chip nForce ...

If in usb controllers nForce what was either a bug, it would have manifested in FreeBSD 7.2
